docs.title: Uri Neri
docs.text:
I am a postdoctoral researcher/data scientist at the Joint Genome Institute, focused on RNA virus discovery and characterization in extreme environments. My work combines bioinformatics, computational biology, and data science to advance our understanding of viral diversity and evolution.

GitHub Twitter Bluesky Scholar

docs.title: Research Focus
docs.text:
Starting May 2024, I will be joining the Joint Genome Institute, where I will work on:

  • In-silico bioinfo tool development
  • Improving characterization of uncultivated RNA viruses
  • Making virus discovery research more interoperable and open

docs.title: RNA Virus Discovery
docs.text:
• Novel virus detection methods
  • Environmental viral diversity
  • Evolution of RNA viruses

docs.title: Haloarchaeal Viruses
docs.text:
• Viral adaptation to extreme environments
  • Host-virus coevolution
  • Structural adaptations

docs.title: Tools & Methods
docs.text:
• Advanced bioinformatics pipelines
  • High-throughput sequencing analysis
  • Custom computational tools
  • Open-source software development
